title: Unix Socket Support
description: Add :unix_socket option to all Vault source modules so the library can connect to a bao agent unix listener

title: Update HTTP.base_request/1 and normalise_response in http.ex
Thread :unix_socket through all 5 source module init/1 functions
Update vault.ex moduledoc with unix socket example
Write unit tests for opts and http unix socket changes
Make token: optional when unix_socket: or agent_mode: true is set
description: In agent API proxy mode, bao agent handles auth — token: should not be required. When unix_socket: is given (or a new agent_mode: true flag), make token: optional. When absent, build_headers/2 in http.ex omits x-vault-token entirely.

title: docs: generated website, API documentation, and user manual
Phase 1: ExDoc audit and completion — rotating_secrets
Phase 2: ExDoc audit and completion — rotating_secrets_vault and rotating_secrets_testing
Phase 3: mdBook scaffold and user manual content
Phase 5: validation — clean build, links, search
Source.Vault.Transit: Pattern B key metadata source
description:
Implement `RotatingSecrets.Source.Vault.Transit` as a Pattern B key metadata source for the OpenBao/Vault Transit secrets engine.

## Summary

Transit keys have no exportable secret material; this source exposes the key's operational metadata to consumers for encryption-key version tracking (rewrap orchestration, min-decryption-version enforcement).

**Vault API:** `GET /v1/{mount}/keys/{name}`
**Material:** `Jason.encode!(body["data"])` — full JSON-encoded data map (consistent with `dynamic.ex:108`)
**Meta:** `%{version: integer, key_type: string, min_decryption_version: integer}`
**Rotation detection:** `meta.version` increments when key is rotated; Registry's change detection notifies subscribers.
**TTL/refresh:** No natural TTL; consumers set `fallback_interval_ms` for polling cadence.

## Also included

- Extract `fetch_required_string/2` and `validate_namespace/1` from 4 duplicate locations into `Source.Vault.Opts` (DRY refactor, 0 behaviour change)
- `HTTP.post/3` deferred: Transit rotation returns 204 No Content, which `normalise_response/1` cannot handle without infrastructure changes; `rotate_transit_key!/2` in OpenBaoHelper uses raw `Req.post!`

Plan: .omc/plans/vault-transit-source.md

title: Phase 1: Extract shared validation helpers to Source.Vault.Opts
description:
Create `RotatingSecrets.Source.Vault.Opts` (`lib/rotating_secrets/source/vault/opts.ex`) with public `fetch_required_string/2` and `validate_namespace/1` and `@spec` annotations.

Replace the 4 private copies in `kv_v1.ex`, `kv_v2.ex`, `dynamic.ex`, `pki.ex` with:
```elixir
import RotatingSecrets.Source.Vault.Opts, only: [fetch_required_string: 2, validate_namespace: 1]
```

Acceptance:
- `Opts` module exists with both public functions
- All 4 existing modules have no private copies
- `mix test` passes (kv_v1_test, kv_v2_test, dynamic_test, pki_test)
- No API surface change

title: Phase 2: Implement Source.Vault.Transit
description:
New file: `lib/rotating_secrets/source/vault/transit.ex`

`@behaviour RotatingSecrets.Source` — implement all 5 callbacks.

`init/1` opts: `:address`, `:mount`, `:name`, `:token` (required); `:namespace`, `:req_options` (optional). Build `base_req` once via `HTTP.base_request/1`.

`load/1`: `HTTP.get(state.base_req, "/v1/\#{mount}/keys/\#{name}")`
- material = `Jason.encode!(body["data"])`
- meta = `%{version: latest_version, key_type: type, min_decryption_version: ...}`

`terminate/1`: `:ok` (no-op; no lease to revoke)
`subscribe_changes/1`: `:not_supported`
`handle_change_notification/2`: `:ignored`

Depends on Phase 1 (Opts module must exist).

title: Phase 3: Unit tests for Source.Vault.Transit
description:
New file: `test/rotating_secrets/source/vault/transit_test.exs`

Follow patterns from `dynamic_test.exs` / `pki_test.exs`:
- `@stub_name :transit_test`, `@valid_opts`, `stub_opts/1`, `transit_keys_response/2` helper

13 test cases:
- `describe "init/1"`: happy path + 4 missing-required-opt errors + invalid namespace
- `describe "load/1 - success"`: happy path (JSON material, meta.version==1), version>1, round-trip JSON decode
- `describe "load/1 - HTTP errors"`: 403, 404, transport error
- `describe "terminate/1"`: returns :ok

All tests use `Req.Test` stubs exclusively. `async: true`, `@moduledoc false`.

Depends on Phase 2.

title: Phase 4: Integration tests + OpenBaoHelper additions
description:
Two files:

**`test/support/openbao_helper.ex`** — add 3 functions (raw `Req.post!`/`Req.delete!`, consistent with existing helpers):
- `setup_transit_engine!(mount \\ "transit")` — mount engine + create `test-key` (aes256-gcm96)
- `teardown_transit_engine!(mount \\ "transit")` — DELETE mount
- `rotate_transit_key!(mount, name)` — POST .../rotate (204 No Content; use raw Req, not HTTP module)

**`test/integration/openbao/transit_test.exs`** — 3 tests tagged `:openbao`:
1. `load/1` returns material with `latest_version == 1` after fresh setup
2. After `rotate_transit_key!/2`, new `load/1` returns version 2
3. Two `RotatingSecrets.register/2` calls on same key return same version

`setup_all` mounts/unmounts engine. Unique names via `:erlang.unique_integer/1`.

Depends on Phase 2.

title: Phase 5: Update vault.ex umbrella docs
description:
Update `lib/rotating_secrets/source/vault.ex` `@moduledoc`:
- Add `RotatingSecrets.Source.Vault.Transit` to module list with description: "Transit key metadata (encryption key version tracking)"
- Add example `register/2` call showing `:mount`, `:name`, `:token`, `:address`
- Note no-TTL behavior: consumers should set short `fallback_interval_ms`

After: moduledoc lists all 5 sources: KvV2, KvV1, Dynamic, PKI, Transit.

Depends on Phase 2.

title: Vault dynamic secret lifecycle: lease renewal, revocation, and PKI source
description:
Improve rotating_secrets_vault handling of ephemeral/transit secrets across three areas:

1. **Lease renewal (Source.Vault.Dynamic)** — before generating a new credential at 2/3 TTL, attempt PUT /v1/sys/leases/renew. Fall back to new-credential generation only on failure. Reduces orphaned credential accumulation in Vault.

2. **Lease revocation on deregister** — implement terminate/1 in Source.Vault.Dynamic to call PUT /v1/sys/leases/revoke fire-and-forget (receive_timeout: 2000ms) when a lease_id is present.

3. **Source.Vault.PKI** — new source for PKI certificate issuance. Parses X.509 not_after for precise TTL. Material is a JSON bundle (cert+key+CA); meta contains only non-sensitive operational fields (serial, expiry, SANs, fingerprint). Optional revoke_on_terminate.

Plan: .omc/plans/transit-secrets-plan.md
Architecture: 5 phases, rotating_secrets_vault only (no core Registry changes).

title: Phase 1: Add HTTP.put/3 to Source.Vault.HTTP
description:
Add put(base_req, path, body) to Source.Vault.HTTP alongside the existing get/2.
- Use Req.put(base_req, url: path, json: body) piped through normalise_response/1
- Wrap in try/rescue Req.TransportError identical to get/2 pattern at http.ex:24
- Add @spec put(Req.Request.t(), String.t(), map()) :: {:ok, map()} | {:error, atom()}
- Unit tests: 200, 403, 404, 5xx, transport-error via Req.Test stubs
Files: source/vault/http.ex, http_test.exs

title: Phase 2: Lease renewal in Source.Vault.Dynamic
description:
Before generating new credentials at 2/3 TTL, attempt PUT /v1/sys/leases/renew.
- Track lease_id and current_material in Dynamic state (both set together on first successful load)
- On renewal success: return same material, update ttl_seconds from renewed duration
- On renewal failure: fall through to existing GET /v1/{mount}/creds/{path}
- Add meta.ttl_seconds = body["lease_duration"] (Vault returns seconds; direct assignment)
- receive_timeout: 5000 on renewal PUT (load path)
- Note: rotation telemetry fires on every load/1 success regardless (Registry constraint, out of scope)
Files: source/vault/dynamic.ex, dynamic_test.exs
Depends on: Phase 1 (HTTP.put/3)

title: Phase 3: Lease revocation in Dynamic.terminate/1
description:
Implement terminate/1 to revoke the current lease on deregister.
- PUT /v1/sys/leases/revoke with body %{"lease_id" => state.lease_id}
- receive_timeout: 2000 (must fit in 5000ms default supervisor shutdown window)
- Fire-and-forget: try/catch, always return :ok
- Do not log lease_id; log only :lease_revocation_failed atom + secret name
- 204 response is treated as :vault_client_error by normalise_response/1 — harmless, do not add 204 clause
Files: source/vault/dynamic.ex, dynamic_test.exs
Depends on: Phase 1 (HTTP.put/3)

title: Phase 4: New Source.Vault.PKI certificate source
description:
New module for Vault PKI certificate issuance with X.509-driven TTL.
- init/1: required opts :address, :mount, :role, :token, :common_name; optional :alt_names, :ttl, :ip_sans, :revoke_on_terminate (default false)
- load/1: PUT /v1/{mount}/issue/{role}, parse not_after via :public_key.pkix_decode_cert/2 for ttl_seconds
- material: JSON bundle {"certificate", "private_key", "issuing_ca", "ca_chain"} — gated behind Secret.expose/1
- meta: %{version: nil, ttl_seconds, issued_at, serial_number, expiry, sans, certificate_fingerprint}
  NO PEM bodies in meta (enforces log-safe meta invariant)
- terminate/1: optional PUT /v1/{mount}/revoke when :revoke_on_terminate, receive_timeout: 2000
- Do NOT implement subscribe_changes/1 (optional callback, Registry uses function_exported?/3)
Files: source/vault/pki.ex (new), pki_test.exs (new)
Depends on: Phase 1 (HTTP.put/3)

title: Phase 5: Update umbrella docs and module index
description:
Documentation-only phase.
- vault.ex moduledoc: add Source.Vault.PKI to module list with minimal register/2 example
- dynamic.ex moduledoc: remove 'PKI' from example use-case list (PKI now has its own module)
Files: source/vault/vault.ex, source/vault/dynamic.ex

title: rotating_secrets: Elixir secret lifecycle library
description: Standalone Elixir library implementing borrow/push secret management with pluggable backends, TTL-aware refresh, leak prevention, telemetry, and strong test coverage. Library is named rotating_secrets with module prefix RotatingSecrets.*. See docs/plans/rotating_secrets.md.

title: Phase 0: project scaffold
description: mix new rotating_secrets --module RotatingSecrets, configure deps (telemetry, file_system optional runtime; stream_data ~> 1.0, mox, local_cluster, snabbkaffe ~> 1.0, observlib dev/test; dialyxir, credo, benchee, ex_doc), .credo.exs, .formatter.exs, CI with dialyzer PLT caching, br init, export plan to docs/plans/rotating_secrets.md, mix deps.get && mix2nix > mix.nix.

title: Phase 9: Memory source
description: RotatingSecrets.Source.Memory — holds value in own source state (no separate GenServer required). init/1: store initial binary value and registry_pid. load/1: return current value from source state. subscribe_changes/1: returns {:ok, channel_ref, state} where channel_ref = make_ref(). handle_change_notification({channel_ref, :updated}, state): returns {:changed, %{state | value: state.pending_value}}. RotatingSecrets.Source.Memory.update(name, new_value): stores new value as pending, then calls send(registry_pid, {channel_ref, :updated}); Registry catch-all handle_info delegates to handle_change_notification/2 → {:changed, new_state} → refresh. No dedicated Registry handle_info clause; message routes through existing dispatch contract. terminate/1: :ok.

title: Phase 11: tests
description: Unit: secret_test.exs (Inspect/String.Chars/Jason.Encoder/Phoenix.Param leak prevention), registry_test.exs (state machine >=90% coverage), supervisor_test.exs, source/file_test.exs, source/env_test.exs, source/memory_test.exs (update/2 sends {channel_ref,:updated} via subscription channel, no Registry bypass), telemetry_test.exs (all [:rotating_secrets,...] events fire, none carry value, :dev_source_in_use fires), log_capture_test.exs (CaptureLog). StreamData properties (_properties_test.exs): rotation_consistency, fail_soft, subscriber_fanout, monotone_versions; generators in test/support/generators.ex. Snabbkaffe resilience (@tag :resilience, NOT async: true): registry crash during refresh, source exception in load/1, subscriber crash mid-fanout, FileSystem watcher crash. Integration: atomic rename triggers reload, concurrent reads during rotation see old-or-new never nil. Multi-node (@tag :cluster, test/cluster/, gated on Phase 10/.14): independent convergence, Horde migration, subscriber cleanup on :nodedown/:noconnection, :pg broadcast, netsplit tolerance, cluster_status/1 with unreachable node. DEPENDENCY NOTE: unit/property/resilience tests can start in parallel with Phase 10 (.14); only test/cluster/ requires .14 complete.

title: Phase 12: documentation
description: Module docs + doctests on all public modules. Conceptual overview in top-level RotatingSecrets module doc. Guides: guides/getting_started.md (Vault Agent + file source), guides/rotation.md (push vs pull, subscriber patterns), guides/security.md (no-zeroize decision, :sensitive flag, leak discipline), guides/writing_a_source.md (Source behaviour), guides/clustering.md (independent-per-node default, Horde via registry_via, :pg cluster broadcast, cluster_status/1, nodedown cleanup), guides/testing.md (Source.Memory.update/2 for in-process rotation, LocalCluster patterns, rotating_secrets_testing companion). specs/README.md (TLC results). ExDoc config in mix.exs with extras: pointing to all guides.

title: Phase 13: quality gates
description: mix dialyzer (no warnings). mix credo --strict (no issues). mix test --cover (>=90% on Registry, Secret, Supervisor). mix test --only resilience (all pass). mix test --only cluster (all pass, requires local_cluster). mix docs (no warnings). mix run benchmarks/current.exs (>=50k ops/s — manual gate; write result to benchmarks/results/reference.md recording CPU model, core count, Elixir version, OTP version, exact command, and measured ops/sec). mix2nix > mix.nix after any dep changes.

title: Phase 10: clustering support
description: Precisely bounded to PRD §9. EXPLICIT NON-GOAL: does not implement cluster-wide state replication or reconciliation (PRD §4). (1) registry_via option in Supervisor/Registry (PRD §9.2): accept {:via, Horde.Registry, MyApp.HordeRegistry} or any via-tuple; library does not depend on Horde. (2) Horde migration correctness (PRD §9.2): on migration new Registry performs fresh init/1 + handle_continue(:initial_load); child_spec must survive :erlang.term_to_binary round-trip (no closures/PIDs/refs). (3) Per-node subscriber cleanup (PRD §9.3): handle_info({:DOWN,_,:process,_,:noconnection}) removes subscriber and emits [:rotating_secrets,:subscriber_removed]; handle_info({:nodedown,node}) bulk-removes all subscribers from that node in <=O(n). (4) Opt-in :pg cluster broadcast default-off (PRD §9.4 SHOULD-level): config :rotating_secrets, cluster_broadcast: true, cluster_broadcast_group: :rotating_secrets_rotations; broadcast {:rotating_secret_rotated_cluster, node(), name, version}; failures must not affect local rotation. (5) cluster_status/1 (PRD §9.5): :rpc.multicall/4 with 5s timeout; {:error,:noconnection} for unreachable nodes; never returns values.

title: Companion: rotating_secrets_vault
description: Plan: docs/plans/rotating_secrets_vault.md

title: OpenBao end-to-end integration tests (Phase V5)
description:
Plan: docs/plans/openbao_integration.md

Live end-to-end integration tests for rotating_secrets_vault against a locally run OpenBao instance in dev mode. Validates the full secret lifecycle (read, rotation, subscriber notification, fail-soft, TTL metadata) against a real Vault-compatible backend.

Module note: integration tests use RotatingSecrets.Source.Vault.KvV2 (per the three-module architecture in the existing vault companion plan), not a single Source.Vault module.

Critical path: task 15.1.1 (core classify_error/1 change) must be committed before any integration tests pass correctly.

title: CI: GitHub Actions workflow with pinned OpenBao binary
Unit tests for Source.Vault with mocked HTTP (no live server)
SourceFault test helper + companion scaffold additions
Integration tests: basic KV v2 reads
Integration tests: TTL metadata from custom_metadata.ttl_seconds
Companion: rotating_secrets_testing (stub)
description: Separate Mix project: rotating_secrets_testing. Depends on rotating_secrets only. Provides: RotatingSecrets.Source.Controllable — a test source supporting manual rotation via RotatingSecrets.Source.Controllable.rotate/2, pushing a new value and triggering {:rotating_secret_rotated, ref, name, version} to all subscribers; RotatingSecrets.Testing helpers module with ExUnit assertion helpers (assert_rotated/2, refute_rotated/2, assert_telemetry_event/2). PRD §5 and §11. This epic is a planning stub: a separate PRD for rotating_secrets_testing is required before full implementation. Minimum scaffold: mix new rotating_secrets_testing, add rotating_secrets as dep, define RotatingSecrets.Source.Controllable with @behaviour RotatingSecrets.Source and stub implementations.

title: Phase 1: TLA+ specification
description: specs/registry.tla — states (Loading/Valid/Refreshing/Expiring/Expired), transitions, invariants (TypeInvariant, NoNilAfterLoad, MonotoneVersions), liveness property. Run TLC to validate. Capture results in specs/README.md.

title: Phase 2: core data structures
description: RotatingSecrets.Secret struct (opaque; Inspect → #RotatingSecrets.Secret<name:redacted>, String.Chars → raise ArgumentError, Jason.Encoder → custom defimpl that raises — NOT @derive; conditional Phoenix.Param defimpl generated when Code.ensure_loaded?(Phoenix.Param) is true at compile time, must also raise). RotatingSecrets.Source behaviour: init/1, load/1, subscribe_changes/1, handle_change_notification/2, terminate/1. Message dispatch contract: sources tag subscription messages with ref from subscribe_changes/1; Registry delegates unrecognized handle_info to Source.handle_change_notification/2.

title: Phase 3: Registry GenServer
description: RotatingSecrets.Registry GenServer: Process.flag(:sensitive, true) first in init/1; non-blocking init via handle_continue(:initial_load); permanent vs transient error classification; state machine :loading→:valid→:refreshing→:valid/:expiring/:expired; TTL×2/3 refresh scheduling; exponential backoff min 1s capped 60s; subscriber Process.monitor with name-and-version rotation notification {:rotating_secret_rotated, sub_ref, name, version} (four elements); handle_info delegates to Source.handle_change_notification/2; Source.terminate/1 in terminate/2. Registry must accept registry_via option for Horde integration; child_spec must be fully serializable (no closures, no PIDs, no refs).

title: Phase 4: Telemetry module
description: RotatingSecrets.Telemetry: all [:rotating_secrets, ...] event names as module attributes — [:rotating_secrets, :source, :load, :start], [:rotating_secrets, :source, :load, :stop], [:rotating_secrets, :source, :load, :exception], [:rotating_secrets, :rotation], [:rotating_secrets, :state_change], [:rotating_secrets, :subscriber_added], [:rotating_secrets, :subscriber_removed], [:rotating_secrets, :degraded], [:rotating_secrets, :dev_source_in_use]. Private emit_* helpers validate no secret value in metadata. attach_default_handlers/0. Library emits raw :telemetry.execute/3 — consuming app calls Observlib.setup/1. test/test_helper.exs sets up observlib for test suite.

title: Phase 5: Supervisor
description: DynamicSupervisor parent; each RotatingSecrets.Registry child: restart :transient, default name via {:via, Registry, {RotatingSecrets.ProcessRegistry, name}}; registry_via option overrides naming mechanism to support {:via, Horde.Registry, ...} for singleton-secret patterns; max_restarts: 3, max_seconds: 30 (surfaces boot-time failures). RotatingSecrets.register/2 → DynamicSupervisor.start_child/2; RotatingSecrets.deregister/1 → DynamicSupervisor.terminate_child/2. registry_via propagated to each Registry child_spec.

title: Phase 6: public API
description: lib/rotating_secrets.ex: current/1, current!/1, with_secret/2, subscribe/1 (delivers {:rotating_secret_rotated, reference(), name, version} on changes only — not on subscription), unsubscribe/1, register/2, deregister/1, cluster_status/1 (@spec cluster_status(name :: atom()) :: %{node() => {:ok, version :: term(), meta :: map()} | {:error, term()}} — uses :rpc.multicall/4 with short timeout; never returns secret values). All delegate to named RotatingSecrets.Registry via :via tuple.

title: Phase 7: File source
description: RotatingSecrets.Source.File. init/1: validate path, warn (not crash) if group/world-readable (PRD §8.1). load/1: File.read! + String.trim_trailing. :file_watch mode: FileSystem watches PARENT directory (not file path) to capture moved_to from atomic rename; filter events by filename in handle_change_notification/2; handle :modified and :moved_to. {:interval, ms} mode: subscribe_changes returns :not_supported. terminate/1: stop FileSystem watcher. Missing file on initial load = error; missing file on refresh = serve stale, log, continue retrying.

title: Phase 8: Env source
description: RotatingSecrets.Source.Env (dev/simple deployments; not recommended for production rotation per PRD §8.2). init/1: emit [:rotating_secrets, :dev_source_in_use] telemetry + Logger.warning with structured metadata. load/1: System.fetch_env (returns {:error, :not_set}, no raise). subscribe_changes/1: :not_supported.

title: Cross-language integration test: OpenBao→Elixir→Rust
description: End-to-end integration test suite proving the full rotation pipeline: OpenBao KV v2 → rotating_secrets_vault (Elixir) → HTTP push → Rust SecretHolder consumer. Plan at .omc/plans/cross-lang-integration.md.

title: Phase 1: Rust http_server.rs example binary
description: Axum HTTP server in secretManager-rs/rotating-secrets-consumer/examples/http_server.rs. POST /secret (mpsc→updater→SecretHolder), GET /secret, GET /health. Stdin-close shutdown via tokio::select!. Prints LISTENING_PORT=N. deny(clippy::unwrap_used,expect_used,panic). Add axum 0.8, serde, serde_json as dev-deps.

title: Phase 2: RustConsumerHelper + :cross_lang skip
description: Elixir test support: test/support/rust_consumer_helper.ex with @moduledoc documenting HTTP JSON contract. start_server!/1 (Port, parse LISTENING_PORT), stop_server!/1 (Port.close), wait_for_ready!/1, push_secret!/3 (flunk on non-200), get_secret!/1. Add :cross_lang skip logic to test_helper.exs (RUST_CONSUMER_BIN / RUST_CONSUMER_SKIP). Per-test on_exit lifecycle.

title: Phase 3: cross_lang_test.exs — 11-step rotation pipeline
description: @moduletag :openbao + :cross_lang, async: false. 11 steps: write v1 to OpenBao → register → subscribe → read Elixir → push to Rust → verify Rust → write v2 → assert_receive rotation → read Elixir → push to Rust → verify Rust. Subscriber-driven wait (assert_receive, 3000ms).

title: Phase 4: CI update — Rust toolchain + pinned secretManager-rs checkout
description: Update .github/workflows/openbao_integration.yml: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable (toolchain: 1.75.0), actions/checkout for secretManager-rs at ref: v0.1.0-integration, cargo build --example http_server --release, RUST_CONSUMER_BIN env var, actions/cache for Rust artifacts.

title: Vault JWT-SVID Authentication
description: Add opt-in JWT-SVID auth support to vault sources via spiffe-ex, with per-load token refresh

title: Add spiffe_ex path dependency to mix.exs
Implement Auth.JwtSvid module (init, ensure_fresh, vault_login, telemetry)
Extend opts.ex with validate_auth dispatch and jwt_svid validators
Thread auth dispatch through all 5 source module init/1 and load/1
Write unit and integration tests for JWT-SVID auth
Phase 1A: Telemetry/logging sanitization (C1, C2, C3)
description: Fix C1: sanitize emit_load_exception/4 at telemetry.ex:111-118. Fix C3: sanitize emit_load_stop/3 error clause at telemetry.ex:107. Fix C2: replace inspect(reason) at registry.ex:306 with format_reason/1. Add sanitize_for_telemetry/1 private helper. Tests written in same phase. See .omc/plans/security-audit.md for acceptance criteria and canary tests.

title: Phase 1B: Input validation hardening (H1, H2)
description: Fix H1: add validate_path_component/1 and validate_path/1 to opts.ex; apply in all 5 source init/1 functions. Fix H2: extend validate_namespace/1 to reject CRLF and null bytes. Property tests written in same phase. See .omc/plans/security-audit.md for acceptance criteria.

title: Phase 2: Medium fixes (M2 req_options, M3 PKI SAN logging)
description: Fix M2: replace Req.new(base_opts ++ req_options) with Req.new(req_options) |> Req.merge(base_opts) in http.ex:12 — confirmed base-wins semantics at deps/req/lib/req/fields.ex:127. Fix M3: replace bare rescue in pki.ex:183-189 with Logger.warning. Tests written in same phase.

title: Phase 3: Low findings + documentation (L1-L5, TLS note)
description: L1: terminate/1 best-effort note in Dynamic and PKI moduledocs. L2: change dev-root-token example in kv_v2.ex:37. L3: && -> and in pki.ex:196. L4: pre-build base_req in KvV1/KvV2 init/1. L5: add current_material comment in dynamic.ex:101. TLS: document Mint verify_peer default in http.ex.

title: Phase 4: Property test suite (8 tests, permanent regression guard)
description: StreamData property tests 1-5 (path/namespace validation, URL invariant, error sanitization). Integration tests 6-6b (telemetry canary for load_exception and load_stop events). Logger canary test 7. Full lifecycle telemetry audit test 8. See .omc/plans/security-audit.md Phase 4 for the complete test matrix.
